name: e2b
description: Manage E2B code-execution sandboxes — list, create, inspect, and kill cloud sandboxes and browse available templates

envVars.name: E2B_API_KEY
envVars.description: E2B API key (starts with e2b_), created in the E2B Dashboard under Keys; same env var used by the E2B SDKs and CLI

tools.name: List Sandboxes
tools.description: List all running sandboxes, optionally filtered by metadata key/value pairs

metadata.description: Metadata filter as a URL-encoded query string, e.g. user=abc&app=prod

tools.name: Create Sandbox
tools.description: Create a new sandbox from a template (starts a billable running sandbox)

templateID.description: Identifier or alias of the template to create the sandbox from, e.g. base

timeout.description: Time to live for the sandbox in seconds before it is automatically killed

autoPause.description: Automatically pause the sandbox after the timeout instead of killing it

metadata.description: Key/value metadata attached to the sandbox (usable as a filter in List Sandboxes)

envVars.description: Environment variables to set inside the sandbox

tools.name: Get Sandbox
tools.description: Get a sandbox by ID, including template, state, resources, and start/end times

tools.name: Kill Sandbox
tools.description: Kill a sandbox by ID, immediately terminating it and discarding its state

sandboxID.description: Identifier of the sandbox to kill

tools.name: List Templates
tools.description: List all sandbox templates available to the account, with CPU, memory, and disk defaults

teamID.description: Identifier of the team to list templates for

tools.name: Pause Sandbox
tools.description: Pause a running sandbox, preserving its state so it can be resumed later — the middle ground between running and killed (returns 204 on success)

sandboxID.description: Identifier of the sandbox to pause

tools.name: Resume Sandbox
tools.description: Resume a paused sandbox with its preserved state and a fresh timeout

sandboxID.description: Identifier of the paused sandbox to resume

timeout.description: Seconds from now until the resumed sandbox expires

tools.name: Set Sandbox Timeout
tools.description: Reset a running sandbox's TTL: the sandbox will expire the given number of seconds from now (each call overwrites the previous timeout) — use this to keep a sandbox alive mid-task (returns 204 on success)

timeout.description: Seconds from now until the sandbox expires

prompts.title: Running sandboxes
prompts.prompt: List my running E2B sandboxes with their template, state, and expiry time.

prompts.title: Spin up a sandbox
prompts.prompt: Create a new E2B sandbox from the base template with a 5 minute timeout and show me its ID.

prompts.title: Clean up sandboxes
prompts.prompt: List my running E2B sandboxes and kill any that I confirm are no longer needed.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: E2B setup

steps.title: Create an E2B account
steps.description: Sign up at https://e2b.dev — the free hobby tier includes sandbox usage credits, so no payment method is needed to test.

steps.title: Get an API key
steps.description: Open the E2B Dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard), go to the Keys tab, and copy your API key (starts with e2b_) or create a new one.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Copy the key into your .env as E2B_API_KEY.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Templates and List Sandboxes to confirm the key works.

notes: The platform REST API is served on https://api.e2b.app and authenticates with the X-API-Key header
Sandboxes are killed automatically when their timeout (TTL) elapses unless autoPause is set
Running sandboxes consume billable compute — kill sandboxes you no longer need
Code execution, filesystem, and process control inside a sandbox are SDK-first (served per sandbox on {port}-{sandboxID}.e2b.app) and are not covered by these platform tools
